stemHtml:
The following thrust formula is given: F = m (vj - vv) + A (pj -p0)

Which answer states the correct explanation of the two parts of the right side of the formula?

options.a: m (vj - vv): the thrust of the hot section. A (pj - p0): the thrust of the cold section of a turbofan.
options.b: m (vj - vv): the thrust of the propeller. A (pj - p0): the thrust of the exhaust of a turboprop.
options.c: m (vj - vv): the thrust of the core section. A (pj - p0): the thrust of the fan section of a turbofan.
options.d: m (vj - vv): the thrust caused by the acceleration of the air mass flow. A (pj - p0): the thrust caused by the pressure difference at the exhaust.
